title: From Genetic Variant to Wet-lab: Accelerating the path from hypothesis to experiment
summary: Google Antigravity is an agentic workbench that provides researchers fluency in scientific tools, models, and databases. It enables rapid workflows from genetic variant observation to wet-lab experiment design through orchestration of frontier models and integrated access to 30+ scientific databases.
writeup: A Google DeepMind researcher demos how an AI agent inside the Anti-Gravity platform compresses a multi-step biology research workflow from hours to minutes. Starting from a single genetic variant in the AK2 gene, the agent queries databases, runs AlphaFold Genome to surface a severe splicing defect as a second hypothesis, retrieves and renders the AlphaFold protein structure, and outputs the DNA sequence blueprints needed to physically test both hypotheses at the bench. The through-line is closing the gap between predictive models and wet lab execution: the agent handles the manual, fragmented coordination work that ordinarily consumes an entire afternoon.
key_points: The AK2 variant initially presents as a missense mutation, but AlphaFold Genome predicts a severe splicing defect, revealing a second distinct mechanistic hypothesis: the cell may misprocess RNA rather than simply produce an altered amino acid.

The agent bridges genomic and protein data without manual hand-off: it predicts a frameshift caused by a misread splice site, then retrieves and renders the AlphaFold 3D structure to show the affected region constitutes a significant portion of the protein.
DNA sequence blueprints for both hypotheses are generated automatically by the agent rather than through manual transcript lookup and fragment design, which previously required an entire afternoon.

The workflow moves from initial observation to testable hypotheses and wet-lab-ready reagent blueprints in minutes, not as a replacement for the bench but as an accelerator that determines which experiments are worth running before committing lab time and resources.

AlphaFold Genome is used here specifically for variant-level splicing prediction, demonstrating the agent's ability to invoke specialized state-of-the-art models on demand rather than relying on a single general model.
